fields.title: Implement /ralph configure interactive TUI
fields.description: Configuring Ralph with command flags is not ergonomic. Add `/ralph configure [task-id]` as the primary way to inspect and edit a Ralph loop's execution contract and pacing configuration through Pi UI widgets.
fields.design: Add a /ralph configure command. Target resolution: if an id/path is provided, configure that Ralph loop; if omitted, configure the loop owned by the current session; if no current loop exists, show a selector of configurable Ralph loops. Build an overlay or custom UI using Pi TUI components (SettingsList/SelectList/Input/Editor patterns) with sections for: Overview, Pacing (max iterations, items per iteration, reflect cadence), Reflection instructions, Execution profile (view pinned mode/model/thinking and explicit recapture from current session), Tools (searchable multi-select over pi.getAllTools with required Ralph control tools locked), Agents (searchable multi-select over AgentRegistry), and Sandbox (view pinned sandbox and explicit recapture). Writes go through the Ralph configuration service. Make scope explicit in the UI header: `Ralph configure: <task-id>`. Keep CLI flags as shortcuts, not the main UX.
fields.acceptance_criteria: `/ralph configure <task-id>` opens an interactive UI for that loop, `/ralph configure` resolves the current Ralph loop or shows a selector, users can edit pacing/tools/agents and explicitly recapture execution or sandbox profiles, required Ralph control tools cannot be disabled, changes persist to .pi/loops/state/<task-id>.json, unsafe active-child edits show a clear warning, and tests cover command target resolution plus at least one persisted UI edit path.
